title: SatForge
description: A from-scratch CDCL SAT solver studio that grew an SMT brain. The SAT core does two-watched literals, VSIDS, first-UIP learning and restarts, emits machine-checkable DRAT proofs of UNSAT and re-verifies them in-browser with an independent RUP/RAT checker, counts models exactly (#SAT, component caching), extracts minimal unsat cores (MUS), and optimizes вҖ” a complete weighted MaxSAT engine (linear SAT-UNSAT and core-guided WPM1 over a Generalized Totalizer) plus encoders for N-Queens, Sudoku, coloring, Hamiltonian cycles, integer factoring (a multiplier circuit run backwards) and the zebra puzzle. NEW: a full DPLL(T) SMT solver built on the very same engine вҖ” SAT lifted to first-order theories. A lazy DPLL(T) loop abstracts a formula's Boolean skeleton, and on each candidate hands the theory atoms to from-scratch decision procedures: EUF (equality + uninterpreted functions) by proof-producing congruence closure with curried applications and minimal conflict explanations; linear real & integer arithmetic (QF_LRA/QF_LIA) by a general simplex (DutertreвҖ“de Moura) over EXACT Оҙ-rationals вҖ” no floating point anywhere вҖ” with conflict explanations and integer branch-and-bound; and mixed QF_UFLIA by Ackermann reduction so uninterpreted functions and arithmetic combine soundly without Nelson-Oppen. Ships a tolerant SMT-LIB 2 parser and an SMT Studio with a curated example library (congruence closure, strict ordering cycles, integer pigeonhole, UF+arithmetic) that shows the verdict, the model, and the exact theory lemmas the solver learned to close the search. NEWEST: a complete QF_BV BIT-VECTOR engine by EAGER BIT-BLASTING onto the very same CDCL core вҖ” every fixed-width bit-vector operation lowered to a from-scratch hardware circuit (ripple-carry adder, shift-add multiplier, restoring division, barrel shifters for shl/lshr/ashr, signed div/rem/mod, comparators, concat/extract/extend/rotate), so the SAT verdict IS the SMT verdict: sound and complete, no refinement loop. A width-checking SMT-LIB QF_BV parser (let, indexed operators, #b/#x/(_ bvN m) literals), a model decoder (hex/bin/unsigned/signed) that re-checks every model against an independent BigInt reference, and DRAT-CERTIFIED UNSAT вҖ” an unsatisfiable bit-vector encoding is re-verified by the existing independent RUP/RAT checker, so the answer is machine-checked. Examples factor integers by running an 8Г—8 multiplier backwards, prove the XOR-swap and the overflow-free average bit trick, and split signed from unsigned order. NEWEST: a complete QF_AX THEORY OF ARRAYS вҖ” McCarthy select/store, constant arrays, and full EXTENSIONALITY вҖ” decided without any new theory solver by REDUCING arrays to the EUF + arithmetic already present: read-over-write purification rewrites every select(store(a,i,v),j) into a fresh element pinned by the McCarthy axioms (recursing through nested writes), surviving reads become an uninterpreted function the congruence-closure engine already handles, and array equality is encoded by a Skolem witness index plus saturated-index agreement clauses (the StumpвҖ“BarrettвҖ“Dill scheme, made finite and ground), so both existing theories inherit arrays for free and QF_ALIA (arrays + integer arithmetic) just works. The SMT-LIB parser learned (Array I E) sorts, select/store and ((as const ...) v); the Studio renders the array contents the solver committed to as per-array cell tables. NEWEST: a complete QF_DT THEORY OF ALGEBRAIC DATATYPES вҖ” constructors, selectors and testers over lists, trees, pairs, enums and Nat вҖ” likewise decided with NO new theory solver, by REDUCING datatypes to the EUF + linear-integer arithmetic already present. The reduction is purely additive: it conjoins the free-term-algebra axioms (exactly-one-tester exhaustiveness + disjointness; constructor pinning is_C(C(вҖҰ)) and sel_i(C(a))=a_i, which yields injectivity for free through selector congruence; the tester link is_C(t) вҶ’ t = C(sel_1(t),вҖҰ) instantiated to a bounded selector depth) plus the one genuinely non-EUF ingredient вҖ” ACYCLICITY BY INTEGER RANK: a fresh rank:DвҶ’Int with rank(t) > rank(child) on every constructor edge, so over a finite set of ground terms a strict > ordering is exactly 'no term is its own subterm' and the existing simplex/branch-and-bound refutes impossible cyclic values like x = cons(a,x). Datatypes thus reduce to QF_UFLIA, Ackermann-combined like UFLIA/QF_ALIA, so QF_DTLIA (datatypes with integer fields) just works. The SMT-LIB parser learned declare-datatype/declare-datatypes (mutually recursive), constructor/selector applications and the ((_ is C) t) tester. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against FourierвҖ“Motzkin, 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an Ackermann+FM reference, 1500 random bit-vector formulas against full assignment enumeration with every operator exhaustively checked, ~3700 random array formulas (non-extensional AND extensional) against an independent finite-model enumerator, and ~1500 random datatype formulas against an independent finite-TREE-model enumerator (every value a real constructor tree, testers reading the real root) that shares no code with the reduction. NEWEST: a complete QF_S BOUNDED THEORY OF STRINGS вҖ” the hardest classic theory (unbounded string equations are undecidable), added in the same style with NO new theory solver, by REDUCING strings (up to a length bound L) to the EUF + linear-integer arithmetic already present. Each string term becomes a length |s| (the reused str.len symbol) plus L integer code-units str.char$(s,k) вҖ” an uninterpreted StringГ—IntвҶ’Int function, so EUF gives congruence for free and Ackermann already combines it with the simplex вҖ” with padding past the end pinned to a sentinel so a string's value IS its code-units. McCarthy-style per-operator axioms unfold str.++ (concatenation), str.at, str.substr, str.contains/prefixof/suffixof and str.indexof over the вүӨL positions into ordinary eq/arith atoms; string equality is kept as an EUF atom AND tied to position-wise code-unit agreement, so '=' means VALUE equality ('ab'В·'c' = 'abc'), not term identity. Because the alphabet is constrained only up to equality, the small-model property makes this sound and complete WITHIN the length bound (so x = 'a'В·x is refuted by the simplex from |x| = 1 + |x|). The SMT-LIB parser learned the String sort, double-quoted literals and the str.* operators, and the Studio reconstructs each variable's solved text. Everything is brute-force certified: 3000 random EUF formulas cross-checked against congruence enumeration, 2500 QF_LRA formulas against FourierвҖ“Motzkin, 1200 mixed UF+arithmetic formulas against an Ackermann+FM reference, 1500 random bit-vector formulas against full assignment enumeration with every operator exhaustively checked, ~3700 random array formulas (non-extensional AND extensional) against an independent finite-model enumerator, ~1500 random datatype formulas against an independent finite-TREE-model enumerator, and ~900 random bounded-string formulas against an independent concrete-string enumerator (plain JavaScript string semantics, sharing no code with the reduction). NEWEST: CRAIG INTERPOLATION and SAT-BASED UNBOUNDED MODEL CHECKING (McMillan 2003) вҖ” SatForge stops reasoning about one formula and starts PROVING WHOLE SYSTEMS SAFE FOR ALL TIME. A from-scratch PROOF-LOGGING CDCL records a resolution refutation (a DAG of partition-tagged input clauses and pivoted resolution steps, down to the empty clause) for every UNSAT, and McMillan's algorithm reads a Craig interpolant straight off it вҖ” a formula I with Aвҹ№I, IвҲ§B unsat, over only the vocabulary A and B share. Built on that, an INTERPOLATION-BASED MODEL CHECKER takes a finite-state transition system (Init, Trans, Bad), runs bounded model checking, and iterates the interpolant of one unrolled step into an INDUCTIVE INVARIANT that proves the bad state unreachable on every execution вҖ” or returns a concrete shortest counterexample trace. A Model Checker studio ships a gallery (a modulo-6 counter, a saturating counter that overflows, a mutual-exclusion protocol and its broken variant, a traffic-light controller, a token ring), each run live with verdict, invariant or counterexample, the interpolation search trace, AND an INDEPENDENT EXPLICIT-STATE BFS REACHABILITY ORACLE (sharing no code with the SAT/interpolation path) shown beside it; plus an Interpolation panel that computes and exhaustively verifies the interpolant of any editable A/B clause pair. Certified the project's way: the proof solver agrees with brute force AND the main engine on 1500 random CNFs (every model verified), ~500 random UNSAT partitions whose interpolants pass all three Craig properties by exhaustive enumeration, and 300 random total transition systems plus the whole gallery where the model checker must match the BFS oracle on verdict, inductive invariant AND shortest counterexample. A SECOND, INDEPENDENT PROOF RULE вҖ” simple-path k-INDUCTION вҖ” runs beside interpolation in the studio, so two independent proofs and the explicit-state oracle must all agree. NEWEST: a SOLVER LAB that turns the engine's heuristics into PARAMETERS (restarts, clause deletion, phase saving, VSIDS decay rate, and a genuinely-uniform random branching mode) and RACES the same proved-correct CDCL core against itself across a reproducible benchmark suite вҖ” random 3-SAT at the Оұ вүҲ 4.26 phase transition, pigeonhole UNSAT, graph coloring and Langford pairings вҖ” scoring the field the way the SAT Competition does: a from-scratch SVG CACTUS PLOT, a PAR-2 leaderboard, and a per-instance speed heatmap, all streamed off the main thread by a dedicated worker. It doubles as a SOUNDNESS ORACLE: because every configuration is the same complete solver with a single knob flipped, they must reach the SAME verdict on every instance they decide, so the panel that shows 'restarts make us faster' also continuously re-proves that no heuristic can make us WRONG вҖ” cross-checked in the harness where all eleven configurations match exhaustive truth-table brute force on hundreds of random CNFs. NEWEST: a from-scratch IC3 / PDR engine (PROPERTY-DIRECTED REACHABILITY, Bradley 2011) вҖ” a THIRD, independent unbounded-safety prover that, unlike interpolation, NEVER UNROLLS THE TRANSITION RELATION. It grows a ladder of over-approximating CNF frames F0=Init вҠҶ F1 вҠҶ вҖҰ using only single-step SAT queries, blocks each reachable bad state by a recursively-discharged proof obligation, learns a blocking clause that it INDUCTIVELY GENERALIZES (Bradley's MIC вҖ” drop literals while the clause stays inductive relative to the previous frame), pushes clauses forward, and declares safety the moment two adjacent frames coincide (their conjunction is an inductive invariant); unsafety is established by a chain of obligations back to Init, with the shortest concrete counterexample materialized by an honest bounded unrolling. The Model Checker studio now shows FOUR verdicts side by side вҖ” interpolation, IC3/PDR, k-induction and the explicit-state BFS oracle, only green when all four agree вҖ” plus an IC3 proof panel with a live frame-ladder, MIC/obligation/SAT-query stats, and PDR's own machine-checked inductive invariant (discovered by a completely different route than the interpolation invariant), and a new maximal-length LFSR gallery example whose 'never latches up at zero' safety is proven by the one-clause invariant registerвү 0. Certified by six new assertions cross-checking PDR's verdicts, invariants and shortest counterexamples against BFS, interpolation AND k-induction on 220 random systems plus the gallery. NEWEST: KNOWLEDGE COMPILATION to smooth d-DNNF вҖ” SatForge stops merely COUNTING solutions and starts COMPILING the formula into a circuit on which a whole family of otherwise-#P-hard queries collapses to a single LINEAR-TIME pass. A from-scratch compiler records the #SAT DPLL search as a shared DAG in smooth, deterministic, Decomposable Negation Normal Form (sd-DNNF): unit propagation becomes an AND of forced-literal leaves, a vanished variable an OR(x,В¬x) coin-flip, independent sub-formulas a DECOMPOSABLE AND (children share no variables), and a branch a DETERMINISTIC OR (the arms disagree on the split variable, so their model sets are disjoint) вҖ” with repeated sub-formulas CACHED and SHARED (the c2d/Dsharp idea) and unsatisfiable branches collapsed, so the circuit is a DAG, not a tree. Because the circuit is smooth+decomposable+deterministic, querying it is exact and linear: an exact BigInt MODEL COUNT, WEIGHTED MODEL COUNTING under arbitrary per-literal weights (the partition function behind exact probabilistic inference, tied to #SAT by Z = count/2вҒҝ under uniform weights), and EVERY VARIABLE'S EXACT MARGINAL PROBABILITY in a SINGLE forward+backward sweep via the Darwiche arithmetic-circuit differential (w[в„“]В·вҲӮZ/вҲӮw[в„“] = WMC(fвҲ§в„“)), the MOST-PROBABLE EXPLANATION (MPE) by a max-product pass, plus model ENUMERATION (each model produced once), independent STRUCTURAL CERTIFICATES that PROVE the compiled circuit really is sd-DNNF, and export to the standard c2d/Dsharp .nnf format. A new Compile studio tab compiles off-thread and shows the verified property badges, a circuit-stats grid, the exact count, and a weighted-inference panel whose TILT SLIDER recomputes the weighted model count, a LIVE VARIABLE-MARGINAL BAR CHART and the most-probable assignment in real time. Certified by seventeen new assertions: over 1200 random CNFs the compiled count matches #SAT and brute force, every circuit is verified smooth+decomposable+deterministic, the weighted count, the one-pass marginals and the max-product MPE match brute force (random and uniform weights), and enumeration equals the exact model set with no duplicates. NEWEST: a from-scratch QBF SOLVER for QUANTIFIED BOOLEAN FORMULAS вҖ” the canonical PSPACE-complete problem, a strict step above SAT/#SAT вҖ” decided on the very same CDCL core by COUNTEREXAMPLE-GUIDED EXPANSION (the RAReQS idea). A QBF is a two-player game between вҲғ (satisfy the matrix) and вҲҖ (break it), and SatForge plays it ONE QUANTIFIER BLOCK AT A TIME: at each block the searching player proposes a candidate move with a SAT call over a growing set of BLOCKING clauses, checks it EXACTLY by recursing on the block-stripped subgame (the chosen move substituted into the matrix), and on failure learns the opponent's winning counter-move вҖ” ruling that candidate out вҖ” until it either wins or the SAT call goes UNSAT and the block is decided; single вҲғ blocks bottom out as plain SAT, single вҲҖ blocks as validity. It returns a DECISIVE CERTIFICATE for the outer block (the winning вҲғ assignment or the refuting вҲҖ assignment) plus a full per-iteration refinement trace and search stats, all under explicit budgets that yield a clean UNKNOWN instead of hanging. Ships a tolerant QDIMACS parser/serializer, scalable families whose value is KNOWN BY CONSTRUCTION (the copy game вҲҖЕ«вҲғИі.вӢҖyбөўвҶ”uбөў that is TRUE because вҲғ reacts and FALSE when the quantifiers swap because вҲғ must commit; a parity ladder over an XOR-is-even matrix that is TRUE iff the innermost block is вҲғ), a seeded random generator, and a new QBF Studio tab вҖ” a QDIMACS editor with a live prefix/alternation readout, the verdict with its BRUTE-FORCE AGREEMENT BADGE, a VERIFIED winning-move certificate (substituted back and re-evaluated by the oracle), search-effort stats, and a colour-coded refinement trace. Certified the project's way: an independent EXHAUSTIVE Shannon-expansion oracle (вҲғ=OR, вҲҖ=AND) the solver must always agree with, headlined by a 1500-instance random cross-check over QBFs of 1вҖ“4 alternating blocks with ZERO mismatches, every returned witness re-verified as a genuine winning move, the copy-game and parity-ladder families at every width, a QDIMACS round-trip and determinism. NEWEST: a from-scratch BINARY DECISION DIAGRAM engine вҖ” the canonical form of a Boolean function, where two formulas are equivalent IFF they compile to the SAME node, so satisfiability, tautology and equivalence all collapse to a pointer compare. A unique-table-interned ROBDD package is built around the single universal operator ite(f,g,h) (a memoized Shannon apply): every connective, cofactor/restrict, вҲғ/вҲҖ quantification, functional compose, exact BigInt model count and cube enumeration are thin wrappers over it. The headline is VARIABLE REORDERING вҖ” the lever that makes a diagram linear or astronomically large: a provably function-preserving reconstruction rebuilds the function under any order (the cofactor identity), and RUDELL SIFTING searches each variable's best level to shrink it (the bit-match function вӢҒбөў aбөўвҲ§bбөў collapses 62вҶ’10 nodes, word-equality 93вҶ’15, while parity stays 2nвҲ’1 in EVERY order). Ships a precedence-climbing Boolean-expression front-end, a gallery of order-sensitive classics, the dual ZERO-SUPPRESSED BDD (ZDD) for set families (union/intersect/difference, count, C(n,k) combinations) and a BDD Studio that draws the live diagram (solid 1-edge / dashed 0-edge) and reorders it on one click. Cross-certified from three sides: a truth-table oracle re-checks every apply, cofactor, quantifier and reorder; a BDD built from a CNF must agree with the project's OWN CDCL solver on SAT/UNSAT and with the #SAT counter on the exact model count; and the ZDD set algebra is checked against closed-form combinatorics and bit-mask set arithmetic. NEWEST: a native PSEUDO-BOOLEAN (0/1 integer-linear) engine that learns in the CUTTING-PLANE proof system вҖ” provably stronger than the resolution behind every other engine here. A pseudo-Boolean constraint is a 0/1 linear inequality ОЈ aбөўВ·в„“бөў вүҘ d (every clause is ОЈ в„“бөў вүҘ 1, every cardinality constraint the unit-coefficient case); the solver propagates by SLACK (a literal whose coefficient exceeds a constraint's slack is forced; negative slack is a conflict) and analyzes conflicts by ROUNDINGSAT-STYLE GENERALIZED RESOLUTION вҖ” reduce the reason that propagated the most-recent literal (weaken its non-divisible, non-falsified literals, then ChvГЎtalвҖ“Gomory divide-and-round so the pivot's coefficient is 1) and add it to the running conflict so the pivot cancels, repeated to a 1-UIP cut that asserts after back-jumping, with a DECISION-CUT fall-back that guarantees termination. It is SOUND BY CONSTRUCTION: every step (add, multiply by a positive integer, divide-and-round, saturate, weaken) is a valid cutting-plane inference, so a reported UNSAT is a real refutation and every SAT model is re-checked вҖ” all coefficients kept exact in BIGINT because cutting planes make them grow. The headline is the famous separation it makes visible: the PIGEONHOLE PRINCIPLE PHPвҒҝвҒәВ№вӮҷ, exponentially hard for resolution/CDCL, is refuted by the native solver in a LINEAR number of conflicts вҖ” PHP(8вҶ’7) in 7 conflicts where the resolution-based CDCL core spends ~1565, shown side by side as a live bar chart. Adds 0/1 OPTIMIZATION by solution-improving (linear SAT-UNSAT) search (knapsack, minimum set cover, the Petersen graph's domination number), a tolerant OPB parser/printer, and a sixth studio that shows the verdict or proven optimum, a cross-check row (native vs. an independent CNF encoding over the project's own Generalized Totalizer + CDCL, vs. a brute-force oracle), the cutting-plane derivation of the first conflict, and a live verification button. Brute-force certified the project's way: the native solver agrees with brute force AND the CNF oracle on 4000 random instances with every model re-verified, optimization optima match the brute-force optimum, the pigeonhole family is UNSAT, and every cutting-plane rule is proven sound over thousands of random constraints. NEWEST: a PHYS STUDIO of INCOMPLETE & STATISTICAL-PHYSICS solvers вҖ” the first engines here that don't prove but SAMPLE, cracking satisfiable instances (even at the phase transition) that stop complete search cold, while never claiming UNSAT. An incremental flip substrate (per-variable clause-occurrence lists; one variable flip updates clause true-counts, the unsatisfied set and break/make counts in O(degree), so tens of millions of flips per second) powers four classic STOCHASTIC LOCAL SEARCH strategies вҖ” GSAT, WalkSAT/SKC, ProbSAT (flip вҲқ cb^вҲ’break, BalintвҖ“SchГ¶ning), and Novelty+ вҖ” plus SIMULATED ANNEALING (energy = unsatisfied clauses, Metropolis flips by e^вҲ’О”E/T, geometric cooling with reheating restarts). The crown jewel is from-scratch SURVEY PROPAGATION: the BraunsteinвҖ“MГ©zardвҖ“Zecchina cavity equations of spin-glass physics run as message passing on the formula's factor graph, computing how FROZEN each variable is across the cluster of solutions, then DECIMATING the most-frozen variables, unit-propagating, and recursing вҖ” finishing the paramagnetic (О·вҶ’0) residual with WalkSAT вҖ” which solves random 3-SAT right at the Оұ вүҲ 4.267 threshold where every complete solver explodes. A live PHASE-TRANSITION explorer sweeps the clause/variable ratio to draw both the satisfiability threshold (P(sat) falling 1вҶ’0) and the easyвҖ“hardвҖ“easy effort peak, deciding ground truth with the complete CDCL solver and timing WalkSAT on the satisfiable instances. A seventh studio renders inline-SVG energy/temperature trajectories, the per-variable survey-field bar chart and the decimation log, a RACE table of the whole field against CDCL as the refereeing oracle (every model re-verified, no stochastic solver allowed to disagree), and the dual-axis phase chart. Certified the project's way: the incremental flip engine is checked against a from-scratch rebuild after EVERY flip, delta predicts the exact energy change, every model re-verifies, no incomplete solver ever reports SAT on a proven-UNSAT instance (incl. pigeonhole), and survey propagation solves under-constrained and near-threshold randoms with always-valid models. NEWEST: a from-scratch CNF PREPROCESSING / INPROCESSING engine вҖ” the simplification layer that, in every competitive SAT solver (SatELite, Lingeling, CaDiCaL, Kissat), runs before and between search and accounts for a large share of real-world speed вҖ” plus an eighth SIMPLIFY STUDIO. It does no search; it rewrites a formula into an EQUISATISFIABLE one with fewer variables and clauses, driving seven rules to a fixpoint: unit propagation, pure-literal elimination, subsumption (drop C when a shorter clause implies it), self-subsuming resolution (strengthen a clause by a resolvent that subsumes it), BOUNDED VARIABLE ELIMINATION (the SatELite core вҖ” resolve a variable away whenever the formula does not grow past a budget), EQUIVALENT-LITERAL SUBSTITUTION (collapse the strongly-connected components of the binary implication graph by Tarjan's algorithm вҖ” which also IS the linear-time 2-SAT decision procedure), and BLOCKED-CLAUSE ELIMINATION. The subtlety that makes this more than deleting redundancies: variable elimination, equivalence substitution and blocked-clause elimination preserve SATISFIABILITY but not MODELS, so each pushes a witness onto a RECONSTRUCTION STACK that reconstruct() replays IN REVERSE to lift any model of the simplified formula back to a model of the original вҖ” the BVE case set by the resolvent argument (choose x's value so one polarity's clauses are satisfied, provably possible because otherwise the resolvent already in the simplified formula would be falsified). The studio shows a DIMACS editor with live technique toggles, before/after variable/clause/literal reduction bars, a per-technique breakdown, an operation log, the simplified DIMACS with a download, and a one-click SOLVE-SIMPLIFIED вҶ’ RECONSTRUCT вҶ’ VERIFY-THE-ORIGINAL round-trip. Certified the project's way: 47 new assertions across the full pipeline AND each rule in isolation вҖ” equisatisfiability cross-checked against the complete CDCL solver and exhaustive enumeration, EVERY model of the simplified formula reconstructed and re-verified against the original (not just one witness), subsumption and self-subsuming resolution shown to preserve the model set BIT-FOR-BIT, and the pigeonhole family kept UNSAT under every technique subset. Part of 496 self-tests in all. NEWEST: a 2-SAT & IMPLICATION-GRAPH STUDIO вҖ” the linear-time decision procedure that lives, almost in passing, inside the equivalent-literal preprocessing rule, promoted to a first-class, fully visual ninth studio. A 2-CNF clause (aвҲЁb) is two implications В¬aвҶ’b and В¬bвҶ’a; collected over the 2n literal nodes, the whole satisfiability question collapses to the STRONGLY-CONNECTED COMPONENTS of that graph (AspvallвҖ“PlassвҖ“Tarjan, 1979) вҖ” UNSAT exactly when some variable shares an SCC with its own negation, otherwise a model read straight off the component order in O(n+m). An iterative, stack-safe Tarjan pass decides and assigns; the studio also reports the EQUIVALENT-LITERAL CLASSES (the non-trivial SCCs вҖ” the same collapse a real solver performs to shrink a formula), the BACKBONE (literals forced in every model вҖ” exactly those в„“ with a path В¬в„“вҶ’*в„“, each with a reconstructed implication-path witness and re-confirmed by solveAssuming(В¬в„“)=UNSAT), and the CONDENSATION DAG with a longest-path layering. It draws the whole implication graph as an SVG coloured by SCC (the contradictory component ringed in red on UNSAT) and the condensation beside it, encodes graph 2-colouring as 2-SAT (bipartite is SAT, an odd cycle is UNSAT), and ships a live PHASE-TRANSITION explorer that sweeps the clause/variable ratio to draw P(sat) falling through the sharp m/n = 1 satisfiability threshold (ChvГЎtalвҖ“Reed / Goerdt), thousands of instances decided in a blink. Certified the project's way against THREE independent oracles вҖ” the complete CDCL solver, solveAssuming, and exhaustive 2вҒҝ brute force вҖ” on the curated gallery, the bipartite/odd-cycle family and 1400 random instances spanning the threshold, agreeing on verdict, model, equivalence classes AND backbone. It also decides the BINARY CORE of an arbitrary-width CNF вҖ” the units + binary clauses, the implication-graph skeleton a real solver propagates over вҖ” as a sound one-way test (core UNSAT вҮ’ the whole formula is UNSAT, confirmed against CDCL over hundreds of random general CNFs; a satisfiable core is inconclusive): 12,215 assertions, all green. NEWEST: a LIA STUDIO вҖ” a complete, STANDALONE decision procedure for QUANTIFIER-FREE LINEAR INTEGER ARITHMETIC (QF_LIA) by the OMEGA TEST (Pugh 1991), a tenth studio and a second, independent route to integer arithmetic alongside the SMT layer's simplex branch-and-bound. Integer linear feasibility is genuinely harder than rational: the rational projection of a variable (its REAL SHADOW) can be nonempty while no integer survives between the bounds. Omega closes that gap EXACTLY вҖ” equalities are removed first by a EUCLID-STYLE reduction (centered remainders shrink the coefficients toward their gcd, mirroring the Euclidean algorithm and never losing an integer solution, with a fast unit-coefficient substitution), then each variable is projected by FourierвҖ“Motzkin refined into the DARK SHADOW (a tightened ОІВ·U вҲ’ ОұВ·L вүҘ (ОұвҲ’1)(ОІвҲ’1) combination that is SOUND FOR SAT вҖ” if it holds an integer, so does the original) and, when the dark shadow is empty but the real shadow is not, a finite family of GRAY-SHADOW SPLINTERS (Pugh's exact-projection theorem, added as equality subproblems) that makes the procedure COMPLETE and TERMINATING. Every coefficient is a BIGINT so a chain of dark-shadow products never overflows or rounds, and every SAT verdict ships a concrete integer model the studio RE-CHECKS against the original constraints (a certificate). A tolerant constraint parser (=, вүӨ, вүҘ, strict < / > exact over the integers, coefficients like 3x or 3*x, comments, multi-constraint lines), an example gallery (the Frobenius / Chicken-McNugget number, BГ©zout lines, dark-shadow gaps, parity contradictions, coin change, splinter-forcing 3-variable systems, one-sided unbounded regions), a live ELIMINATION TRACE, and an INDEPENDENT EXHAUSTIVE-INTEGER ORACLE shown beside every verdict. Certified the project's way: ~900 assertions вҖ” 600 randomized BOUNDED systems where exhaustive integer brute force (the box IS the whole feasible region, so it decides both SAT and UNSAT) must match the Omega verdict exactly, every SAT model re-validated against the raw constraints, plus a battery of hand-derived classics and a separate 4000-instance stress cross-check with ZERO mismatches and every model verified вҖ” sharing no code with the decision procedure. NEWEST: the LIA Studio grew THREE major capabilities вҖ” INTEGER LINEAR OPTIMIZATION, a 2-D SHADOW view, and FULL PRESBURGER ARITHMETIC. (1) It now OPTIMIZES a linear objective over the integer lattice by a LINEAR SATвҖ“UNSAT DESCENT on the Omega test вҖ” anchor a feasible incumbent, repeatedly assert f вүӨ incumbent вҲ’ 1, each SAT tightening it and each UNSAT certifying optimality вҖ” with EXACT UNBOUNDEDNESS detection: the objective runs to ВұвҲһ iff the recession cone holds an integer ray d with AВ·d в–· 0 and cВ·d вүӨ вҲ’1, itself a QF_LIA query answered by a SECOND Omega call, returned as a concrete improving ray. (2) For a two-variable system it draws the INTEGER LATTICE classified feasible/infeasible, the rational feasible polygon (the REAL SHADOW) by SutherlandвҖ“Hodgman half-plane clipping, every constraint line, the integer shadow projected onto each axis, and the optimum with its objective level line. (3) It decides FULL PRESBURGER ARITHMETIC вҖ” first-order linear integer arithmetic with arbitrary вҲҖ/вҲғ ALTERNATION, a strictly larger language than QF_LIA ('every integer is even or odd' is a Presburger truth no quantifier-free query can state) вҖ” by a from-scratch COOPER'S ALGORITHM: normalize the eliminated variable's coefficient to Вұ1 (re-variabling x вҶҰ в„“В·x, which manufactures a divisibility в„“ | x), form the вҲ’вҲһ formula, and return вӢҒ_{j=1..Оҙ}(FвӮӢвҲһ[j] вҲЁ вӢҒ_{bвҲҲB} F[b+j]) over the lower-bound set B and the divisor lcm Оҙ, with вҲҖx.ПҶ handled as В¬вҲғx.В¬ПҶ. Divisibility predicates are first-class, closed sentences collapse to вҠӨ/вҠҘ, and OPEN formulas come back as an equivalent quantifier-free divisibility condition the studio prints and lets you test against a value. A tolerant formula parser (вҲҖ/вҲғ/forall/exists, вҲ§ вҲЁ В¬ вҶ’ вҶ”, d | term divisibility) and a curated gallery (even-or-odd, no greatest integer, residues mod k, quantified Chicken-McNuggets, Frobenius as an open formula). Cross-certified TWO independent ways: Cooper's decisions match the OMEGA TEST on hundreds of existential conjunctions AND an exhaustive BOUNDED EVALUATOR on hundreds of box-guarded вҲҖ/вҲғ sentences and open-formula eliminations (where the box makes brute force a complete oracle), the optimizer's optima match exhaustive integer search over bounded boxes, and the 2-D lattice agrees with both brute force and the Omega verdict вҖ” ~4,150 new assertions, all green. Over 17,000 assertions across the whole project, all green.
